lede: A look at November 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ever Anderson and Tyler Hansbrough, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2020 United States presidential election
events.desc: The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump. On November 7, Biden was declared the winner.

events.title: Abdullah Çatlı
events.desc: Abdullah Çatlı, the leader of the Turkish ultranationalist organization Grey Wolves, dies in the Susurluk car crash, leading to the resignation of Interior Minister Mehmet Ağar (a leader of the True Path Party).

events.title: Greensboro massacre
events.desc: Greensboro massacre: Five members of the Communist Workers Party are shot dead and seven are wounded by a group of Klansmen and neo-Nazis during a "Death to the Klan" rally in Greensboro, North Carolina, United States.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: Vietnam War: U.S. President Richard M. Nixon addresses the nation on television and radio, asking the "silent majority" to join him in solidarity on the Vietnam War effort and to support his policies.

events.title: Suez Crisis
events.desc: Suez Crisis: The Khan Yunis killings by the Israel Defense Forces in Egyptian-controlled Gaza result in the deaths of 275 Palestinians.

events.title: Air India Flight 245
events.desc: Air India Flight 245 crashes into Mont Blanc while on approach to Geneva Airport, killing all 48 people on board.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Koli Point action begins during the Guadalcanal campaign and ends on November 12.

events.title: Russian Civil War
events.desc: Russian Civil War: The Russian Army retreats to Crimea, after a successful offensive by the Red Army and Revolutionary Insurgent Army of Ukraine.

events.title: Constitution of the Netherlands
events.desc: A greatly revised Dutch constitution, which transfers much authority from the king to his parliament and ministers, is proclaimed.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon's armies are defeated at the Battle of Vyazma.

deaths.text: Dick Cheney, American businessman and politician, 46th Vice President of the United States
Henri van Praag, Dutch philosopher, theologian, and educator
Solomon R. Guggenheim, American art collector and philanthropist, founded the Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum

observances.text: Christian feast day: Acepsimas of Hnaita and companions (Greek Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Elias I of Antioch (Syriac Orthodox Church)
